citations.description: bd80 ticket comment: 'FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed'

findings.criterion: All child tickets (P1, P2, observability, test-hardening, Jira-cleanup) are closed.
findings.detail: All named child tickets are individually closed (1f3d, 4b59, 48c8, bd80). However, the epic ticket itself remains in 'in_progress' status, not 'closed'. The test-hardening work was folded into P1/P2 rather than filed as a separate ticket, which is consistent with the session handoff comment. The bd80 (Jira-cleanup) ticket was closed via FORCE_CLOSE without a completion-verifier signature, bypassing the normal gate. This is a procedural gap but the substantive work is attested in the ticket comments. The epic's own status not being 'closed' is the primary gap against this AC.

citations.description: search_files for 'artifacts/jira-cleanup' in repo root returns no matches
bd80 comment: 'Backup: all 13 tickets fully backed up (222MB... at $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira-cleanup/backup/ BEFORE any deletion'
bd80 comment: 'DRY-RUN COMPLETE (read-only, no deletions) — all 13 tickets, artifacts in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira-cleanup/'

findings.criterion: The cleanup backup artifact + dry-run log exist before any live deletion.
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable per the rules), but the backup artifact and dry-run log referenced in the bd80 ticket were created in '$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira-cleanup/' — a transient job directory, not committed to the repository. No 'artifacts/jira-cleanup/' directory exists in the repo (search returns no matches). The artifacts are referenced only in ticket comments (operator attestation), not verifiable from the codebase.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
